title: Box::leak audit, removal, and CI prevention gate
purpose: After B-050 (constant pool for literals) and B-051 (process-heap BIF allocation) land, all known Box::leak sites should be eliminated except the legitimate atom-name intern in atom/table.rs. This brief performs a full audit, fixes any remaining sites missed by the two prior briefs, adds a CI-checkable grep gate to prevent new leaks, and verifies the fix with a long-running memory-stability test.
task: Audit every Box::leak call in crates/beamr/src/. Fix any remaining sites not covered by B-050 or B-051 (e.g. code_management_bifs.rs, code_management.rs, or any new code added between briefs). Consolidate the duplicate code_management.rs / code_management_bifs.rs files. Add a verification script or test that greps for Box::leak and fails if any are found outside atom/table.rs. Add a memory-stability integration test that runs a BIF-and-literal-heavy workload and asserts no monotonic memory growth.

requirements.title: Full Box::leak audit
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL audit every Box::leak call in crates/beamr/src/ and categorise each as: (a) already fixed by B-050/B-051, (b) needs fixing in this brief, or (c) legitimate (atom/table.rs only).
acceptance: A grep of 'Box::leak' across crates/beamr/src/ is performed
Every match is categorised: fixed (B-050/B-051), to-fix (this brief), or legitimate (atom/table.rs:136)
The audit covers test files too — Box::leak in test helpers is acceptable but should be documented
No uncategorised Box::leak site remains

requirements.title: Fix remaining Box::leak sites
requirements.spec: IF any Box::leak calls remain in non-test production code after B-050 and B-051, THE SYSTEM SHALL replace them with the appropriate allocation mechanism (process heap via ProcessContext, or constant pool, or removal if the leak is unnecessary).
acceptance: Every remaining Box::leak in production code (not tests, not atom/table.rs) is replaced
Code paths that previously leaked now use ProcessContext helpers or other owned allocation
Any Box::leak in code_management_bifs.rs or code_management.rs is fixed
grep for 'Box::leak' in crates/beamr/src/ (excluding tests/ and atom/table.rs) returns zero matches

requirements.title: Consolidate duplicate code_management files
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL consolidate code_management.rs and code_management_bifs.rs into a single file. IF both files contain overlapping BIF implementations, THE SYSTEM SHALL keep the more complete version and remove the other.
acceptance: Only one code management file exists under native/ (either code_management.rs or code_management_bifs.rs, not both)
All code management BIFs (load_module, purge_module, delete_module, check_old_code, check_process_code) are in the surviving file
The module is re-exported from native/mod.rs
No dead code or duplicate function definitions

requirements.title: CI-checkable Box::leak prevention gate
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include a test that greps for Box::leak in crates/beamr/src/ and fails if any matches are found outside atom/table.rs and test code.
acceptance: A #[test] function in a new or existing test file runs a grep-equivalent check
The test uses include_str! or std::process::Command to search the source tree
Matches in atom/table.rs are explicitly allowed
Matches in #[cfg(test)] blocks are explicitly allowed
Any new Box::leak in production code causes test failure with a descriptive message
The test is in the default test suite (runs with cargo test --workspace)

requirements.title: Memory-stability integration test
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include an integration test that runs a BIF-and-literal-heavy workload and verifies no monotonic memory growth over time.
acceptance: Test spawns a process that loops N times (e.g. 10_000), each iteration: call rand:uniform/0, build a tuple, build a list, materialise a literal, send a message to self
After the loop, trigger a full GC on the process
Assert: process heap size after GC is within a reasonable bound (not proportional to N)
Assert: no panic, no OOM, process exits normally
Test is in crates/beamr/tests/ and runs with cargo test --workspace

boundaries: SHALL NOT re-implement what B-050 and B-051 already fixed — this brief covers residual sites and verification only
SHALL NOT remove the Box::leak in atom/table.rs — atom name interning is permanent by design (ADR-002)
SHALL NOT remove Box::leak from test code — test helpers may legitimately leak for simplicity
SHALL NOT implement a custom global allocator for leak detection — the grep gate and memory-stability test are sufficient
SHALL NOT change any term layout or allocation semantics — this brief is cleanup and verification
verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests plus new audit/stability tests
grep for 'Box::leak' in crates/beamr/src/ (excluding atom/table.rs and #[cfg(test)] blocks) returns zero matches
The memory_stability.rs test shows no monotonic memory growth
Only one code_management file exists under native/
All code management BIFs remain functional
